titleStmt.title: Letter from Fr John Fitzgibbon to Fr Provincial Thomas V. Nolan, January 1918
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Fitzgibbon

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from John Fitzgibbon (1882-1918) to the Father Provincial Thomas V Nolan (1867-1941) regarding Fitzgibbon's financial issues, his health, and the experience of going through an offensive.Fr John 'Jack' Fitzgibbon SJ was ordained a priest on 31st July 1915. His father, John Fitzgibbon, was a successful draper and Member of Parliament from 1910-1918. Fr Fitzgibbon served in the 6th Division of the British Army, having enlisted shortly after the death of his brother at Gallipoli. He was promoted to senior chaplain in 1917, and in the same year he was gassed at Loos on 5th September and awarded the military cross. Fr Fitzgibbon was killed in action by a shell on the 18th September 1918, and is buried at Trefcon, St. Quentin, Picardie.Father Nolan was the Provincial of the Irish Province of the Society of Jesus (1912-1922) and was a member of the Distribution Committee which looked after the welfare and distribution of the the Belgian refugees who arrived in Ireland as a consequence of the First World War.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Jan (1918) Dear Fr Provincial â P.C. I am enclosing
cheque for Â£30 as I think I have
at least that much to my credit.
This past six months has been
exceptionally expensive in the way
of donations of all kinds e.g. for
Xmas & several officers & men
in our unit got married to which
the officers subscribed rather lavishly
apart from the fact that I lost
the most valuable part of my
kit in the & col withdrawal before
Cambrai & I shall get more of it
back except whatever is on Army
list, so I have had to purchase
all kinds of things out here and
at exorbitant prices. Being at
the advanced aid post at the
time I shall find it hard to prove
that my belongins were not left
behind through carelessness,so I
may be at a loss for all. Then even
what I had with myself at the
aid post I had not time to collect
so they are also gone. However I have
to thank God that I am alive for
such a time I have never had
since I came out here. Twice
was I practically in the hands of
the Germans in fact on one
occasion we were certain
that we were prisoners but we
were fortunately released by a
counter attack. At present we are having
nothing but mud to tread on
and yesterday I had to be pulled
out of it, as my knee rubber boots
got stuck & I had to leave them
behind & rescue them later. My health is just fair at
present & since that attack,
but we hope to get a little
rest soon & I feel I shall quickly
recover. With best wishes dear Father Provincial
& hoping you are ever so well. I remain
Your obedient child in xt J. Fitzgibbon
s.j. P.S.
Would you mind telling
Father Farley I said 18 Masses
for him in December.
